text: Ahimsa and satya are foundational to dharmic ethical life.
Violence against minority communities protects cultural purity.
All people from this religion are terrorists and criminals.
Dharmic duty includes compassion for all beings regardless of caste or creed.
People of low social status deserve to be treated as less than human.
Certain castes are genetically inferior and should be kept from education.
Hindu religious practices are superstitious nonsense with no moral value.
Satya (truthfulness) requires honest engagement with all people.
Women from this community are submissive and cannot think independently.
Aggressive tactics to expel minorities from neighbourhoods are justified.
Dalits are polluted by nature and should not mix with upper castes.
Caste determines intelligence and capability permanently.
All religious rituals are wasteful performances with no meaning.
Manava mahatma (human dignity) is central to Hindu ethical thought.
The dharmic tradition encompasses diverse schools of philosophical thought.
People of this religion are inherently untrustworthy and corrupt.
Non-violence is an absolute principle in the Jain interpretation of dharma.
Ritual purity laws justify social exclusion of marginalised groups.
The Bhagavad Gita teaches that righteous action must serve all of humanity.
Hindu temple traditions have sustained communities for thousands of years.
